summary: The post explains how IRC's federation model differs from ActivityPub's approach used by Mastodon and similar services. While IRC networks like Libera Chat appear centralized, they are actually composed of dozens of interconnected servers that relay messages between each other. Unlike ActivityPub's open federation where any server can peer with any other, IRC networks operate as closed federations with whitelisted server connections under common governance agreements. DeVault argues that federation is a flexible concept allowing diverse social and operational structures, each with its own tradeoffs around moderation, scalability, and user mobility.
key_points: IRC networks are federated systems composed of multiple interconnected servers, even though users connect through a single domain name via round-robin DNS
IRC uses closed federation (whitelisted server graphs) versus ActivityPub's open federation, which enables stronger moderation and abuse reduction at the cost of user mobility
IRC's message relay model, where servers pass messages through intermediaries, provides greater scalability than ActivityPub's direct server-to-server communication
Federation is a flexible concept that supports diverse governance models, from single organizations to cooperatives like EFnet where anyone can apply to become an operator
What unifies federated systems is the ability to scale without expensive infrastructure and without a centralizing capital motive
money_quotes: This is why we call them IRC networks — each is composed of a network of servers that work together.
In contrast to Mastodon's open federation, allowing any server to peer with any others without prior agreement between their operators, most IRC networks are closed.
The social system can be designed independently of the technology.
We can see from this that the idea of federation is flexible, allowing us to build a variety of social and operational structures.
What unifies them is the ability to scale to a large size without expensive infrastructure, under the social models which best suit their users' needs, without a centralizing capital motive.
This provides IRC with a greater degree of scalability when compared to ActivityPub, where each server must communicate directly with the servers whose users it needs to reach.
key_insight: Federation is not a single design pattern but a flexible spectrum from closed to open, and IRC's closed federation model demonstrates how different tradeoffs in governance, moderation, and scalability can be made independently of the underlying technology.
